yield_curve.assessment: Normal upward sloping curve with healthy steepness suggesting no imminent recession concerns.

fed_policy.assessment: Fed funds rate appears appropriately positioned with real rates near neutral given 2.67% core PCE.

credit_spreads.assessment: Extremely tight credit spreads signal peak complacency and excessive risk appetite in credit markets.

inflation.assessment: Inflation expectations anchored near Fed target while core PCE at 2.67% suggests persistence above target.

bull_case: Fed may need to cut if 7.45% GDP growth proves unsustainable and credit markets stress emerges. Current real yields above 1.8% provide attractive entry point if growth falters.
bear_case: Explosive 7.45% GDP growth with core PCE at 2.67% suggests economy running hot while credit spreads at 3bps indicate dangerous complacency. Duration vulnerable to inflation surprise and credit normalization.
brief_for_cio: SHORT DURATION: 7.45% GDP growth, 2.67% core PCE, 3bp HY spreads scream overheating economy and peak complacency. Position for higher rates and credit spread widening via TBT/PST.

bull_case: VIX at 29.49 indicates stress that could drive flight-to-quality flows into Treasuries. Previous overheating conditions may have cooled.
bear_case: Previous analysis showed extreme overheating (7.45% GDP, tight credit spreads). If conditions persist, duration remains vulnerable to higher rates.
